mission: Build Nigeria's crash-intelligence and emergency-response spatial operating system, beginning with a Lagos-Ibadan Expressway pilot.
non_negotiables: Public map records must display provenance and verification level.
Demonstration data must never be presented as verified crash data.
Victim names, phone numbers, number plates, and graphic evidence must not be public.
FRSC 122 must remain the primary emergency escalation action.
AI may assist review but may not declare fatalities or legal fault.
The interface must work on low-bandwidth mobile connections.
All spatial records must include coordinate accuracy and timestamps.

public: Severity, date, verification, and corridor filters

pilot.north_star: Reduce report-to-verification-to-escalation time and generate actionable prevention intelligence.

definition_of_done: A user can report a crash with GPS coordinates.
A verifier can review, merge, reject, or confirm the report.
A responder can update the operational status.
The public can view only privacy-safe incident information.
The system produces corridor hotspots and intervention-ready evidence.
